mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in payroll employment above 150k signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods with PAYEMS >150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 5d momentum reverses direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth and housing starts indicate weak demand conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in demand contraction scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or GDP-housing correlation breaks down.

mechanism_narrative: A 10bp increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals tightening monetary policy, increasing borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-FEDFUNDS environments are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or rate hikes fail to correlate with market declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly increase in industrial production signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or supply-demand imbalances emerge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer prices and retail sales signals weak demand, prompting central bank intervention and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-inflation-low-retail scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or policy responses fail to materialize.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low-unemployment environments are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or wage growth fails to correlate with market declines.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and declines in unemployment indicate overheating supply-side conditions, prompting rate hikes and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in overheating scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or supply-demand dynamics reverse.
